challenges.title: Semantic Layout
challenges.description: Create a page layout using semantic HTML5 elements: header, nav, main, and footer. The header should contain an h1, the nav should contain a list of links, the main should contain a paragraph, and the footer should contain a paragraph. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a semantic page layout -->
<!-- Use header, nav, main, and footer elements -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<header' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <header> element'
assert '</header>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <header> element'
assert '<nav' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <nav> element'
assert '</nav>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <nav> element'
assert '<main' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <main> element'
assert '</main>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <main> element'
assert '<footer' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <footer> element'
assert '</footer>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <footer> element'
assert '<h1' in html.lower(), 'Header should contain an h1 element'
hints: Use <header>, <nav>, <main>, and <footer> instead of generic divs
These semantic elements describe the purpose of each section
Place an <h1> inside the <header> and navigation links inside <nav>

challenges.title: Article Page
challenges.description: Create a full page layout with header, nav, main (containing two article elements each with at least one section), an aside, and a footer. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a full semantic page -->
<!-- Include header, nav, main with articles/sections, aside, and footer -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<header' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <header> element'
assert '<nav' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <nav> element'
assert '<main' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <main> element'
assert html.lower().count('<article') >= 2, 'Should contain at least 2 article elements'
assert html.lower().count('</article>') >= 2, 'Should close all article elements'
assert html.lower().count('<section') >= 2, 'Should contain at least 2 section elements'
assert html.lower().count('</section>') >= 2, 'Should close all section elements'
assert '<aside' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an <aside> element'
assert '</aside>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <aside> element'
assert '<footer' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <footer> element'
assert '</footer>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <footer> element'
hints: Place <article> elements inside <main> to represent self-contained content
Use <section> inside each <article> to divide it into themed groups
The <aside> element is for content tangentially related to the main content
